Camille Arno, Seš'qa's Agaš'mekanaz (Keeper of Wisdom), sought a partnership with Endfield Industries in a personal capacity. Following a recommendation from the Endministrator, he currently holds a position within the Specialist Tech Division. 

Operator Camille's parents were members of the joint science expedition from Terra. He himself was born and raised in Seš'qa. However, due to the requirements of his job and his past experiences, Camille has lived outside the floating city for many years. 
Although Camille claims to be "hunting" a criminal kin, his extremely long yet blank curriculum vitae suggests that he has quietly swapped his roles of "hunter" and "prey". The entire corpus of his social relations, personal background ... or his "life", if we were to adopt a broader definition, has been completely destroyed across a period of several decades. 
When I picked this up in the discussion, Camille averted his gaze to the ceiling and stared at a blotch. He then broke the silence with a stream of metaphors and figures of speech. One of them, which I felt was most impressive, was how he described himself as a "pendulum working in vain". 
He went on and said there was a certain day when his time stopped ticking. 
When the conversation was over, I walked away with the most important lesson when engaging with this operator — If you wish to avoid the excessive task of emotional management, DO NOT talk about his sibling. 
— Martin Marvin Malen, Assistant, HR Division, Endfield Industries

added.2801591303690895052:
"Number 81! Oh dear, you're being naughty again." 
He placed a fist-sized heat lamp on the table, poked at the top that was still warm, then watched it roll across the table. It was the naughtiest of the kids. Unlike Number 35, who could not stop squabbling, or Number 29, who was quiet but always scheming for a major prank, Number 81's delinquency came from the bottom of its heart, a living heart — Number 81 never wanted to be a heat lamp with a screw fitting. It did not intend to give off constant and small amounts of heat for a service life that seemed like an eternity. It did not like warming the plants growing on the horticultural shelves of the auto-farm, be they Buckflowers, Tartpeppers, or the like. That was why Number 81 kept breaking down. Its glow was either too dim or too bright. Of course, it was just acting up and could still be salvaged. Number 81 lacked the courage to simply rebel against its function. 
"You won't explode, but you won't splutter out and go dark either, you little coward," the man poked Number 81 some more and it rolled back to its original place. "What in the blight can you do?" 
Maybe it could get a round of cutting and transform itself into an important component of an Arts Unit. Or so Number 81 thought. But that was not its fate. It belonged to Great-Grandfather, who was once a thermal conductance module of a proper Arts Unit. The ancient component witnessed the failing of the Cosmic Gate, the hosts of the Aggeloi that moved like a great tide, and the filthy blood spilled on the Land when the humans killed each other... It was a legend, a witness of an era of suffering, one that once trod upon the scars of humanity. 
Being an Arts Unit would not work. Was there another path? Perhaps an aiming scope? It started thinking again. But that was not Number 81's destiny. Grandfather walked that path. It was the sights of a large-caliber firearm. The master who accompanied it left the sealed subterranean strongholds and roamed the wilderness. Like a powerful scavenger that gnawed at the bones, they crushed and pulverized the remnants of the Aggeloid host. Then they went north, reached the Aurora, lamented in disappointment, and abandoned themselves upon the solid ice that never melts. 
So once again Number 81 racked its mind. What about an image projector? But it quickly extinguished the idea. That was Father. Its main corpus once emitted patterns of light cast onto a wall to give form to maps and images of people. It once gave detailed depictions of the crazed warlord who had made a huge mess of things. Eventually, it also showcased the shattered aftermath of his mad ambitions. The people were exuberant and they celebrated the conclusion. 
With ancestors that served various illustrious functions, Number 81 felt only pain knowing that its generation was reduced to plant nurturers in the auto-farm. It had been excited when it first arrived. But one year later, the strings of regret began to tie it down. Pallets of fresh auto-farm produce would be its only contributions to this world. Standardized production processes, standardized supplies, and standardized praises... The place seemed to have cut a hole in its heart, and blood poured out of it every day that seemed the same as the day before and would be the same as the day after. Its empty heart seized its throat, suffocating it during silent nights, choking it awake, and giving it a sense of hopeless resignation that thickened and deepened over time. 
It knew it was sick. There was something pale and empty that kept trying to erase its memories. The UWST slogans of productivity, industry, and revival became nothing more than empty noises that sank into this pale nothing... And the pale nothing poured out of its orifices, covered its face and body, and made it frightened of looking in the mirror. It could feel shame. In a time of relative peace, it would have restrained its inner uncertainty and set it aside as an ennui of a coddled and spoiled entity. But this sensation lingered and could not be ignored. 
Something was missing. Something that kept Grandfather going until it bled itself dry, that supported Father through its exhaustion... It was something called duty, and none of it remained in Number 81. It once fantasized about a day where some hero would deliver a radio broadcast, invite every light bulb to an epic enterprise, ask them to ignite themselves, and with this great action, banish the deepest darkness from Talos-II. But this was just a fantasy. No new tales of the Endministrator had surfaced in this Land for years. 
So the man gently placed Number 81 down. He then took a few deep breaths to suppress the emptiness that sank into his chest, pushed open the door, headed out into the open, and gazed upon the night sky. 
The place was a wide expanse and there were no clouds in the skies, but no stars either. Just a beautiful view that could fit snugly in a tomb.

added.-7545888493238850091:
"A soldier who enjoys weapon forging is considered an elegant lover of his trade. Forcing a smith, who had no mind for conflicts, to swing a sword is considered a mistake. An error of the time, or fate." 
Whenever my old mentor said these words, he would stare at the incomplete design blueprint of the greatsword. I knew he missed my brother senior again. 
My mentor and senior peers spent seven years on the blueprint. In those seven years, the bits of frost that graced my mentor's sideburns grew till his hair turned gray. My brother senior, once proud and ambitious, became taciturn. Only my sister senior kept smiling, like spring with its warm breeze. The original schedule only required our brother senior to design the crossguard and we could submit the prototype blueprint for review. We were certain it would be exemplary. 
But the day never came. Not even after their names were removed from the rosters of the Hongshan Swordmancers. 
It was autumn. My brother and sister seniors were deployed to the frontlines as technicians overseeing the delivery of a cache of weapons and equipment. It was a treacherous path and my brother senior never wanted to go. His true passion lay in the smithy, forging, and machining, and he loved the sight of red hot metal ingots, the sound of hammering, and the high-pitched hums of spinning lathes. He never had talent for swinging the finished weapon against the enemy. Indeed, nobody ever saw him wield an actual weapon. 
But since my sister senior agreed, he set aside the incomplete blueprint and traveled north with her. The trip was long. My brother senior only returned, alone, when Hongshan received its snow. As for my sister senior ... they laid her to eternal rest in the deep gorge of the Aggeloi. 
Since his return, my brother senior would not stop describing a dream he kept having. He was caught in a narrow mountain trail where the winds cut like razors, and an Aggelos sank its horn through my sister senior's chest. The blades were scattered all around him, yet he could not even pick up any of them to protect his companion. 
He never brought himself to finish the prototype's design. Within months, he left the Bureau of Swordmancers without a word. 
We tried looking for him. My mentor especially. He chased after rumors and suspected sightings, but they turned out false. Sickness seized the old man and he never found the strength to travel again. As he breathed his last, his withered hand clung to the unfinished blueprint that clearly bore the drawings and writings of three swordsmiths. The crossguard remained blank. 
After my mentor passed away, we continued to receive reports about my brother senior. Someone claimed to have seen a bartender with a strong likeness at La Fantoma, one who would get himself sickeningly drunk when the hour grew late, one who got himself fired from every bar of the city. Another sighting described an inept fighter at the Asphalt Roundabout who was thrown out of the ring by an impatient opponent. There were also descriptions of a shaggy homeless man lying on the tracks of Triglava with dirt in his nails. The Durin laborers tried to move him and saw a Yanese red knot in his clothing... 
Fortunately, none of these were really him. 
Because my brother senior returned during an evening when red maple leaves were being shed by their trees. 
We identified him by his threadbare clothing, for he had grown horrendously gaunt, his face sunk deep, and his eyes looked stagnant until he focused, where his gaze turned sharp like chiseling blades. He came back from the northern front. He fought for years but did not become a powerful warrior as he had hoped. Instead, he became a common rank-and-file wrecked by chronic pain. After returning to the Bureau, he demanded a workshop and the old blueprint. We found it, covered in dust, and handed it to him. 
His first strokes were clumsy and painful to watch. It was obvious that old wounds suffered through the years had healed poorly and made his writing hand tremble awkwardly. I had to hold down his elbow and arm so he could start writing and drawing normally again, though it was extremely exhausting and he was soon drenched in sweat. Yet he did not stop and kept working as though the day was his last. He drew and he wrote through the entire night, and never took a break. 
Ten days passed and the blank of the blueprint was finally filled and completed. One year later, that greatsword entered mass production. A weapon measuring 5 feet and 1 inch long, with a width slightly over 8 inches, a weight of 79 catties, and an output powerful enough to smash an Aggelos into gravel. It was lauded as one of the most successful products released by the Bureau of Swordmancers in the last ten years. 
My brother senior did not accept any accolade for it, for immediately he headed back north after completing the design and never came back. We eventually received a purchase order from the Order of Steel Oath bearing the familiar hand of my brother senior, an order for that greatsword whose design he completed. Later, he fell in that battle, the one that everyone read about in history.

"Jakub's Legacy" is part of my childhood memories. Reed rye flour seasoned with sizzling hot energized aggagrit, kneaded, baked, then sliced and lightly panfried. Every bite was a resounding crunch... They said the Warlord Jakub left this recipe. But food is food, and food is never wrong.
Carly and the kids came running when they heard about chow. All the young ones were giggling when they had this. They described it like eating dried little twigs, then said it felt warm as though the wind itself was casting the Arts. Finally, they licked their lips and said: "Valley IV got sneaky and placed a little game in my foodie bowl."

added.-7541464209703653214:
Endministrator: 
Greetings.
This letter serves two purposes.
The first is to express my gratitude. My fowlbeasts have been lethargic for quite a while. My colleagues and I made use of your delivery to fix the fowl pen, and now they are doing much better. Absent your delivery, the poor fowls might never see the Nongye Tianshi for their routine health checkup. 
As for the second purpose: I often see the AIC Factory you deployed right opposite our facilities and I have always wanted to help and ease your burdens. Ever since I arrived at Wuling, I've done nothing but fowl-raising, and I am desperately unskilled in other fields. However, if you have some time for leisure, perhaps you can swing by my place for some nice tea, a gentle breeze, and a quality break. 
Best regards,
And good health to you.

added.-7114915545979303637: This area is often the subject of short fiction and novelettes in Wuling. The popular series titled "Twin Heroes of Yan" chose Sword Vault Dale as the site where the hero buried the sword and retreated into seclusion. Other short stories often depict the vault as a mystic sanctuary filled with numerous weapon blueprints and long-lost technologies. However, the actual conditions of both the Sword Vault Dale and the secret vault were known to only a few. The site also became buried under the Blight following the outbreak of the First Aggeloi War, and almost all writings that mentioned the place were lost in the chaos that followed. Historical records mention how "the Ministry of Rites and Ministry of Works jointly constructed a secret vault at this site", and this was used as a reference by historians from the Hongshan Academy of Sciences to speculate that the dale-bottom vault may contain copies of intricate technologies or heavy machinery that could help explore and develop Talos-II.

<@intru.bold>- Participation Requirements -</>
<@gray.note>· Endministrator Authority Level ≥ 30</>

<@intru.bold>- Event Rules -</>
<@gray.note>· During the event, you can select any combination of Test Criteria before starting the combat challenge.</>
<@gray.note>· The combat challenge has a total of 4 waves. Defeat all enemies to beat the challenge. If you fail the challenge, the challenge must be re-attempted from the beginning.</>
<@gray.note>· Test Criteria are a series of effects that make the challenge more difficult. They are divided into these difficulty levels: 1<image="Common/icon_contingency_contract_entry_black" width=30 height=30> / 2<image="Common/icon_contingency_contract_entry_black" width=30 height=30> / 3<image="Common/icon_contingency_contract_entry_black" width=30 height=30>. Total Test Criteria is the sum of difficulty levels of all selected Test Criteria.</>
<@gray.note>· Test Criteria are also divided into regional "Criteria Sets". Complete challenges at higher Total Test Criteria to unlock more Criteria Sets.</>
<@gray.note>· Some Test Criteria are [Locked Criteria] that cannot be selected by default. Select the specified [Key Criteria] to unlock them.</>

<@intru.bold>- Event Rewards -</>
<@gray.note>· Complete event mission goals to earn rewards such as Vitrified Coins. Successfully completing a Test Criterion for the first time also rewards Vitrified Coins.</>
<@gray.note>· Vitrified Coins can be exchanged at the [Secret Sanctuary] for rewards.</>

<@intru.bold>- Sanctuary Rating -</>
<@gray.note>· [Sanctuary Rating] represents your current best challenge record in terms of Total Test Criteria. Sanctuary Rating will be displayed at Friends-related screens.</>
<@gray.note>· Successfully completing a challenge with Total Test Criteria of at least 1<image="Common/icon_contingency_contract_entry_black" width=30 height=30> activates [Sanctuary Rating] for the event.</>
<@gray.note>· [Sanctuary Rating] for this Contingency Contract event will only be active for the duration of the said event.</>

<@intru.bold>- Event Updates -</>
<@gray.note>· After event launch, an update will be applied on day 8 at 12:00 (server time). This adds a new batch of Test Criteria. The new Test Criteria cannot be selected if their Criteria Set is still locked.</>
<@gray.note>· After event launch, another update will be applied every 3 days at 04:00 (server time) to update and add 1 cycle mission.</>
<@gray.note>· All Contingency Contract missions will be available until the end of the event.</>

<@intru.bold>- End of the Event -</>
<@gray.note>· This is a limited-time event that will end at the specified time.</>
<@gray.note>· After the event ends, combat challenges can no longer be attempted. Mission progress will not be updated as well.</>
<@gray.note>· After the event ends, the [Secret Sanctuary] will remain open for a while so that the remaining rewards can be exchanged. Once the [Secret Sanctuary] closes, all unused Vitrified Coins earned during the event will be removed.</>

Endministrator: 
Thanks a lot for the delivery! It helped me identify an issue with Xiranite yield. There had been quite a few unexpected incidents when Xiranite yield would drop to really low levels, and we kept failing to identify the core issue. Guess what happened next? A Tianshi saw the goods you put on sale and asked me to order some for him. They turned out to be crucial in our endeavors! I have faith that once we fix the problem, we can then hook our Xiranite production facilities to your PAC and stabilize Xiranite production efficiency! 
Thank you once again, Endministrator.
Best regards.

The judges and other tasters of this dish have arrived at a unanimous conclusion: The aromas of jincao and redjade ginseng created a natural harmony within this clear infusion. Wholesome, soothing, and possessing a distinct, layered taste, this soup imbued warmth throughout the body.
However, a flaw existed in this otherwise perfect dish — the stubborn, lingering bitterness of redjade ginseng. The condiments used contradict the herb's pharmaceutical properties. We believe food should be therapeutic, but this dish fell short in that respect. Nevertheless, the infusion remained a revitalizing masterwork.

Physical DMG Dealt <@ba.vup>+{phy_dmg_up:0.0%}</>.

When the wielder applies <#ba.crush>Crush</>, the wielder gains Physical DMG Dealt <@ba.vup>+[{phy_dmg_up2:0.0%} + ({atk_up_mult:0.0%} × Max number of <#ba.noguard>Vulnerability</> stacks <#ba.consume>consumed</> from one enemy)]</> for {duration2:0}s.
<@ba.info>The two effects apply separately and do not stack with themselves.</>

The judges and other tasters of this dish have, after lengthy deliberation and multiple rounds of discussion, arrived at a unanimous conclusion: This dish is the perfect symbol of the Endministrator's past memories. The presentation was unique. Though only mildly seasoned, the texture was compact and uniform, a package of energy and flavors compressed into ready-to-eat portions. A mysterious condiment was used, lending the rations flavors entirely new to us. If this condiment were made available on the market, it would add plenty of delightful culinary possibilities to Wuling.
Unfortunately, the works of other contestants were equally astonishing. This entry also tasted similar to standard military provisions, with the condiments being the only highlight.

added.-5367183257751147747:
Mi Fu is the Watchguard Captain of the Wuling Scientific Developmental District under the Hongshan Academy of Sciences (HAS). She is currently an exchange visitor at Endfield Industries. The Specialist Tech Division is responsible for her reception. 

Captain Mi Fu claimed the purpose of her visit was to see a few old friends from Endfield. But since her arrival, it has become obvious that she has been studying and judging every operator by their combat prowess. On several occasions, she said these words softly to operators she came across: "Interested in sparring with me?" When Mi Fu saw our training cabins, she raised an eyebrow and became interested at once. After experiencing the facilities offered, she expressed: "I would have been here ages ago if I knew the place offered great fights like this!" And immediately extended the duration of her visit by a few more days. In any case, we hope her exchange visit to Endfield will be as enriching and entertaining as possible. 
— Martin Marvin Malen, Assistant, HR Division, Endfield Industries

added.-5354325440520586621:
You're still torturing me. 
I know it very well. This is my 1299th attempt to forget everything about you and your seductions, but I failed... We have been so close, and kept each other company for so long. How could I ever cast these memories aside...? 
Oh beloved left hand of mine. 
A year has passed since I had to amputate you for getting Blighted in the material science experiment. But I can still feel your existence. I can still feel your feedback whenever I stretch or curl the absent fingers or wave the bygone palm. The physician called it phantom pain and a common ailment. All I needed was to set my mind to rest, and maybe accept a few treatment courses and therapy sessions. But I know a quack when I see one. I know their kind well. 
We were only separated and I have not lost you. That is certain. I know where you are, although I am temporarily unable to reach your side. 
You are in the Ætherside. 
...... 
Ætherside, my love. The greatest source of trouble that we have faced together, back when we were still students and after we joined the Portable Æthertech Labs. The Ætherside has always been influencing our lives. Is there anyone else who knows more about the Ætherside than us? Probably not... Wait, no... I think they do exist, the Endfielders ... those working in the best labs of the TGCC and UWST ... and the strange ones who roam the wilderness but have a knack for it. They also have great knowledge for the Ætherside too. 
This is why I am confused, dear. My inability to find you made me realize that I hardly know anything about the Ætherside. Nothing living should exist there. I shouldn't be feeling you at all, but the gentle sensations emanating from the stump where you were severed felt so clear and real. Part of my mind is fighting another. The conclusion has caused the corpus of my knowledge, one that I have meticulously constructed over the years, to utterly collapse. 
I think I need to head out a little. 
...... 
They have begun to inject me with sedatives. 
...... 
I went looking for a former classmate. We grew up and studied together, and spent many years trying to one-up each other in our work and the impact factor of our research projects. He was shocked at the shallow and obvious nature of my question... I only asked him for his views on the Ætherside. Instead, he answered my question with another question: How do I regard the rising of the sun and the falling of the moon? Or the four seasons? I told him I am part of the seasonal cycle, and part of me rests in the rising sun and falling moon. 
He then called the police. The public safety officers of La Fantoma arrived quickly. 
...... 
I believe I have arrived at a UWST auto-farm. Convincing the ex-Oathkeeper security guard that I am not a Landbreaker in disguise proved quite a challenge. He shared some food and we sat before a stove, toasting with our good right hands. I asked him the same question. Fury, hatred, and a sprinkle of cowardice came to his eyes. He emptied his cup and then told me a story he heard when he was a boy. It was a tale about killing the ocean. 
The fire burned in his chest. I cannot let this fire harm you. Fortunately, I carried some sleeping pills with me. 
...... 
I can feel you very clearly now. 
...... 
The Archosauria woman sat within a formation of denstacks. Hah. She thinks her performance is impressive, but I saw right through her. I've used you to flip through numerous tomes to read about the conquerors and sovereigns whose names were noted down by history... I know the lies spat out by her kind of people. Listen to her voice, the empty shells of promises she sugarcoats. She treats the Ætherside as a tool... Of course, I might've been seeing things due to my extreme hunger. Anyway, if her theatrics and performance deceive me, then she's really good at what she's doing. 
...... 
I can wait no longer. I am going to find you. That Lei Fa thunder-casting Tianshi has not noticed me as I infiltrated the Exclusion Zone... I should be able to find a passage that leads to your location. 
...... 
The colors are so beautiful. I must enter it. It's time to go ... and become one with it.

added.-4838437933790607992:
Camille always thought his emotions were well-hidden away. When compared to other operators who exercised zero restraints, he did a fine job. Unfortunately, unintended movements and barely noticeable body language still betrayed him. 
When frustrated, Camille would lower his gaze and study a crack, if any, on the ground, or subconsciously rub his shoulder blade. When he wishes to avoid contact, he would shift his center of balance to his right leg to expedite immediate flight. When hesitating or discomforted, his fingers would subconsciously rub the old, ancient key hanging from his waist. 
Many Endfield operators have an item or keepsake they focus on. For example, Yvonne treats Frost-e-Bytee as a close friend, Rossi has her dagger, and Da Pan is always carrying that ferrium wok on his back. But Camille is different. If anyone were to ask him about his key, he would immediately retract his hand as though the thing had become a burning brand. He would keep his lips tightly closed and refuse to respond to the inquiry. 
Skilled machinists would identify that the key was a handcrafted from something that resembled a music box cylinder, though Camille knows nothing about artisanry. The man has no property to his name, and no one knows what the key could actually open. 
Eventually, everyone became wiser and learned not to ask about the item. 
In the end, Camille confessed to the origins of the key during a casual conversation. It was a present personally crafted by his younger brother (the very same who broke all ties with him). It was part of their home at Seš'qa, which Camille later sold after an accident and no longer returned to. The key was merely ornamental and his habit of touching the key was rooted in longing ... and perhaps a dash of inexplicable ennui. After saying these words, Camille placed the key in one of his pockets. 
What he remembered was the searing pain as if a chunk of flesh were being torn from his body, and a dark and exhausting past that went on and on. The gift was offered when sincerity remained. The Keeper faced a dilemma, and he no longer wanted to be reminded. 
A few days later, Camille received a Baker toast and visited the Endministrator's office. He was received by a smiling Endministrator who dropped a new key into his hands. 
"Such keys and locks are pretty rare, so making them took quite a while," the person said, "I just want you to know that Dijiang always has a door that you can open." 
It caught Camille by surprise. He tightly wrapped his fingers around the key and turned his face aside. He tried to form words, but they came out like noises made by a wheezing fowl whose neck was caught in an iron grip. In the end, he only managed to blurt out a barely audible phrase: "Thank you." 
Not everybody is familiar with Camille's body language, but everyone knows that the jingling of the new key against the old is joyous, like how he felt at that very moment.

added.-4576453353590151871:
Keep casting Mi Fu's battle skill to unleash 3 different moves. The first battle skill cast is <@ba.key>Cloudtrapper</>.
<@ba.key>MOVE 1 - Cloudtrapper</>: Costs <@ba.vup>100</> SP and <#ba.return>returns</><@ba.vup> 50</> SP after casting; Mi Fu fires a grappling chain from her gauntlets that deals <@ba.pd>Physical DMG</> to the target and nearby enemies, then attempts to pull the target and nearby enemies towards her. After casting MOVE 1, the next battle skill will be temporarily replaced with <@ba.key>Trail and Mangle</>.
<@ba.key>MOVE 2 - Trail and Mangle</>: Costs <@ba.vup>50</> SP; Mi Fu unleashes a flurry of punches that deals <@ba.pd>Physical DMG</> with the final punch applying <#ba.crush>Crush</>. If this Crush <#ba.consume>consumes</> at least 3 <#ba.noguard>Vulnerability</> stacks from a single target, then the next battle skill will be temporarily replaced with <@ba.key>World Splitter</>.
<@ba.key>MOVE 3 - World Splitter</>: Costs <@ba.vup>50</> SP; Mi Fu deals massive <@ba.pd>Physical DMG</> to a specified area; this DMG is not treated as Battle Skill DMG, but as <#ba.crush>Crush</> DMG.

added.-3675220694833104674:
The Sarkaz sat in a circle upon the soft, mushy snow. They had discussed the same problems multiple times for the past few months. Tactics for dealing with the ubiquitous Aggeloi, how many soldiers they should send to search and rescue the other races who were inept in fighting, and how they should live with the collapse of the Cosmic Gate and the fact that they would never see Kazdel again. 
The Aggeloi were like an unending flood. The swarming host covered every road, forced them out of the northern frontier outposts, and sent them on a desperate retreat. Once again they became roamers. A people without a future in sight, and without a way back to their homeworld. 
The sunlight reflecting off the great rings of the gas giant grew dimmer by the day. The walls became increasingly caked in blood, and food reserves ran low. The air grew stale and suffocating as fewer people had ideas to share. The Sarkaz remained silent, even when the Samifjod shared their goodwill. 
But this day was different, thought Fleming. Everyone must say something, so he broke the silence first. "Do we have a conclusion on that Feranmut tooth? Turn it into another Lifebone?" 
"I don't get it. Why give us a thing of such great value?" 
"Because we offered them crucial military support, maybe?" 
There was a brief pause. "But the Feranmut fell anyways." 
"Their Snowpriests mentioned a prophecy about us..." 
Confusion and uncertainty still saturated their words, but there was progress, however slow it was. Fleming tried to direct the flow of the discussion in the way he intended, and he did so by tweaking the topic. "There's no need to overthink it. This planetoid has a vile temper, one that forces people to work together if they wish to survive. And our relations with the Samifjod go way back. This isn't just about us. The others need the Lifebone as well. We must return north, win this war, and save more people... Our only consideration is how we can secure the blessing." 
When the "north" was mentioned, the Sarkaz fell silent once more. Nobody knew how long this war was going to last, and they were already tired and sick of it. 
Curses. A mistake. 
Fleming wiped the sweat from his forehead. The best sales pitch should never, ever reveal its bottom line so quickly. While he squeezed out a smile and frantically prepared another topic to revive the discussion, a Vampiress sitting furthest away, one that Fleming hardly knew, whose name even escaped his memories, took the lead. Elegantly she leaned against her thorny spear of blood red carmine, and spoke in her gentle voice that Fleming was familiar with. 
"Why? Don't any of you look forward to this? Tell me how many Sarkazian cities did we have back on Terra?" she purred as moldable blood glistened and rippled in mid-air as it slowly took the form of a city. "And now we have this distant planet filled with mysteries. Imagine, if you can, the possibility of us forging an era of peace, and think of the Talosian land and skies — I believe the time has come for us to raise our new home." 
"A floating city that cruises through the heavens upon the bones of the Feranmut. It must have its own style, and a powerful name." Fleming heard himself talk. 
"What about New Kazdel? Wait, that sounds too big... How about New Furnace?" 
"It would need a ring of permanent artillery and defensive works. Don't want no flying buggerloids striking from the skies!" 
The discussion turned solemn, somber, and stern... The people's faces revealed their longing for the new city that they would soon be building with their hands. The hesitation and uncertainty that reigned over the emotions of the Sarkaz were dispelled, and an unprecedented urge of impatience took their places. While the crowd clamored and argued, someone blurted out the word — "Seš'qa". A word without the prestige or greatness most envisioned, a place to serve a simple purpose. A midway stopping point between the road forward and the road home. 
But it was a beautiful word. Fleming juggled it on his tongue and mulled over the delicate tremors felt by his lower mandible, given force by the pulmonary exhalation required to lend voice to the word. An idea slowly crept into his mind — Why should the Sarkaz be a dark, brooding people with a penchant for violence and seek only warfare as their companion? No. The new city should be a theme park with a giant wheel ride, open-air bazaar, theaters, and every element of fun that could fit, and it did not matter if the element was not part of the Sarkazian past. 
The northern winds cut like razors, and it was the frigid cold that kept Fleming's mind in a state of crystalline clarity. His imaginations went wild. He had events in mind. "Lift-Off Day", "Open Day", "Parade of the Royal Courts" — festivals that the Sarkaz could use to gently land upon this planet, like a dandelion seed. 
This new city would not be blanketed in smoke from the funeral pyres for the fallen warriors. Instead, it would have something far more beautiful, like fireworks to bless its existence. 
It did not matter what the others thought. The Speaker on the other side of the Cosmic Gate should love this idea. She would be happy whenever someone mentions anything about explosives. 
Fleming would have many chances to prove his skills as a merchant. The next course of action would be to sell this idea.

Research this to construct <@tech.key>Xiranite Relays</> and <@tech.key>Xiranite Pylons</>.
<@tech.key>Xiranite Relays</> can auto-connect to the Protocol Automation-Core (PAC) and any type of Xiranite power facilities within 80 meters. When placed inside the Core AIC Area, they can auto-connect to the PAC regardless of distance. Xiranite Relays can be used for power transmission and can supply power to nearby facilities.
<@tech.key>Xiranite Pylons</> can auto-connect to the PAC and Xiranite Relays within 80 meters; and to Xiranite Pylons and other power facilities within 30 meters. When placed inside the Core AIC Area, they can auto-connect to the PAC regardless of distance. Xiranite Pylons can be used to supply power to facilities within range and transmit power across short distances.

DOCUMENT: HR Promotion Records of Wuling Scientific Developmental District 
NAME: Mi Fu 
REGION OF ORIGIN: Wuling 
WORK EXPERIENCE: 3 years 
CURRENT POSITION: Wuling Watchguard 
PROPOSED PROMOTION: Wuling Watchguard Captain 

CURRICULUM VITAE & BACKGROUND: 
Mi Fu was a Qingbo Stockade resident who was later recruited by the Wuling Scientific Developmental District, and was made a watchguard after the completion of Wuling City. Since assuming the role, she has constantly worked at the frontlines, demonstrating extensive knowledge regarding security issues within Wuling City and the peripheral regions, as well as decisiveness and alacrity when handling various emergencies and cases. Mi Fu is committed to Wuling City, has zero tolerance for any form of delinquency or criminality, and always acts with strict justice. She has a good reputation, exercises effective deterrence, and serves as a rallying point for the general public. 

STRENGTHS: 
Exceptional combat prowess. Impressive physicals and fortitude. Capable of handling high-risk threats. 
Decisive and skilled in executing orders. Capable of making the right calls when facing complex situations. 
Caring and nurturing towards those working under her. Capable of forming a strong, cohesive team. 
Good reputation among the general populace and has earned their trust and support. Public reviews include: "A little blunt but always just." 

AREAS TO IMPROVE ON: 
Over eagerness in applying force. Tendency to employ physical measures when resolving various situations. Lack of procedural awareness. 
Slight disadvantage in clerical work such as report drafting. Limited administrative skills. 
Quick tempered. Lacks patience for communication. Prone to conflicts with others. 
Gives inordinate trust to familiar individuals. Decision-making may be affected by personal relationships and emotions. 

OVERALL RATING: 
Mi Fu has delivered remarkable results in patrol work and law enforcement. Having successfully quelled multiple emergencies, she is a promising officer who can be relied upon for field work. However, she inclines toward blunt approaches that lack finesse, has poor procedural awareness, and runs the risk of violating the proper chain of command. Nevertheless, the general public is very satisfied with Mi Fu's performance. The candidate has made positive contributions, although mistakes are not absent. Further guidance and strengthening of her self-restraint will be required. 

OPINIONS OF THE REVIEWER(S): 
Mi Fu has a fiery disposition and a great passion for upholding justice. Though she often acts out of turn, her actions are never meant for ill. She swings her fists not for herself, but for the safety of the people. Callousness is a mere outcome of her straightforwardness. Occasions of procedural non-compliance and lack of clerical perfection only hide, not cancel, her meticulous attention to detail. She may fall short in such areas, but they are also where she excels. 
I am willing to vouch for her character and good standing, and to recommend her for promotion to Watchguard Captain of Wuling City, where she will become the mainstay of the city's public safety. I will also personally supervise and guide Mi Fu in her daily work. 

REVIEWER(S): Zhuang Fangyi

added.-478942484917990241:
"Endmin! Please help me keep Mi Fu occupied for a day!" It was late at night when Yan Ning paid a sudden visit to the Endministrator for an extremely urgent business. 
According to Yan Ning, she saw a sealed letter to be sent to Mi Fu while she was cleaning Zhuang Fangyi's desk. She thus took it upon herself to mail it to the Watchguard's barracks. 
But she quickly learned that the letter was not meant to be delivered at once. In Yan Ning's words: "If Mi Fu reads the letter before the hour intended by Fangyi, things could get really bad! Please keep Mi Fu distracted while I intercept the letter's delivery!" 
And hence, the Endministrator used the excuse of "live auditing" and kept Mi Fu company for the entirety of her daily shift. 
It began early in dawn when a traveling merchant surrounded by the Aggeloi called for help. Captain Mi then spent the entire morning mercilessly training the new watchguards. In the afternoon, she had to arbitrate disputes between shop owners of Fangxing Avenue followed by an unexpected encounter of the "Supreme Chief" at the bamboo grove... Mi Fu's fists never rested, yet they remained effective as always. 
Her restless shift finally ended when the sun has finally set and night took over. Still the Endministrator remained as Yan Ning has not yet intercepted or retrieved the letter. 
Back at the watchguard barracks, Mi Fu turned on the desk lamp and began filling in the procedural files and records under its warm, yellow glow. The wind outside made whistling sounds. She suddenly lowered her pen and headed outside, but saw nothing but loose leaves carried in the breeze. The Captain had felt rather restless on this day. 
Mi Fu returned to her desk and closed the case files. From her pocket she took out a letter from the Main Pillar Office, creased and soaked in sweat. She had signed and accepted it in the morning but never found time to read it. 

DOCUMENT: A Letter Not Intended for Delivery 
Mi Fu: 
I'm probably already on my way by the time you read this letter. In the event that I do not make it back, I will be counting on you to handle all public safety and administrative affairs of Wuling City. 
You have accompanied Wuling in every step. You were there when it was just a simple construction site in the wilderness and stayed till it became a city with beautiful buildings and orderly roads. You toiled with the construction crew, stood the night watch, and kept trouble at bay with your fists. I know your ties to Wuling run deep, and this is why I entrust this heavy duty to you. 
You never shied away from duty or responsibility. That is your strength. But your temper lacks patience and is obstinate. I understand this is part of your character, but the task of defending a city goes beyond fighting off enemies and winning battles. It requires tact and mindfulness. Deliberating and acting with prudence are not signs of weakness, but measures taken to guarantee success and give me a peace of mind. 
There is also the matter of Qingbo Stockade. This subject pulls you taut and tense. Meeting fellow Stockaders seems to ignite your fuse, but I know you have been struggling to act with mercy. Some people might not know the truth, but I see it clearly. It is nothing to be ashamed of. You have every right to be a friend of Tangtang without feeling the need to keep it a secret. 
And remember when I advised you to practice calligraphy? At first I was hoping it could improve your patience, but your words turned out just as stern and unyielding as your fists, and assumed a beauty of their own. The lessons are no longer necessary, but of course, if you find calligraphy enjoyable and have the leisure of time, seek Tianshi Ge for advice. There is no need to bring Tangyou Baba to him. His health no longer allows them in his diet. 
You often said how you had enough sweets for your lifetime and never wanted to see them again because sweetness now makes you sick. But I believe otherwise. There is no need to avoid what you like. Duty is heavy and taxing. Rare are the opportunities of personal happiness, and tiny indulgences should be kept available. A piece or two every now and then will do no harm. 
Finally, I have a few personal words to say to you. What I wanted to give you most is not this city, but yourself. You were a young girl with a fiery gaze when I first met you. In a single blink you charged straight for me with a fist poised to strike. But years have passed. You took on greater responsibilities, your fists became more merciless, and your frowns never eased. I have not seen your true self for a while. 
So remember, if there comes a day when you feel unhappy and work becomes a chore, then just leave it at that. Do not feel beholden to anyone and just ignore what the others say. Go wherever you like. Head back to the Stockade or travel across Talos-II. You can even choose to do nothing. You owe Wuling nothing, and thus you should be free. 
Take care of yourself. Smile and laugh when the time is right. 

Zhuang Fangyi

added.143173027379210516:
DOCUMENT: The Tourist's Guide to Talos-II: Seš'qa, Issue 3 
Seš'qa Open Day is here! What should a wise tourist do? Visit Witching Hour's flagship store? Buy yourself a limited edition of something not offered on the shelves of TGCC shops? Sip on something nice at the Glasshouse Bar? Look for secret witch-crafter ateliers in the dark alleyways? There are just so many things to do in the floating city. Our latest guide gives you a secret and nuanced choice: Book a visit to a safe house of an Agaš'mekanaz, or Keeper of Wisdom. 
I used to treat these Keepers as a mysterious group of dangerous and merciless individuals who work in the shadows. I believe many readers share my view. The organizers of recent Open Days might be interested in changing this stereotype, since they added more Keeper-related activities to the agenda to help us learn about their long-term contributions and good work. 
My guide was a retired Keeper. A kind and patient Goliath, she was nothing like the cold and merciless Agaš'mekanaz I had in mind. She told me that after Jakub Meissen betrayed Seš'qa, the city experienced a crisis of trust, even though the event also devastated Sarkazian communities of Talos-II. To prevent such things from happening again, the leaders of Seš'qa gathered the very best and established the office of the Agaš'mekanaz. Their focus is to handle malicious crimes perpetrated by, or targeting, the Sarkaz. 
We spent the next hour happily walking past evidence walls, filing cabinets, and showcases of various specialized witchcraft equipment. The convo we shared taught me a few secrets. The biggest challenge that any Keeper could face would be crimes committed by a fellow kindred. Generally speaking, the other races would balk at putting a relative of the suspect on the case, but the Sarkaz took the opposite approach. Many of their tribes are blessed with long life and mastery of a wide selection of Originium Arts. Hence, a kindred bound by blood also happens to be the perfect counter to Sarkazian criminals. 
"This is why many of the Agaš'mekanaz suffer emotionally. Some doubt our ability to remain just and fair, but none of us have yet to abandon principle for a kindred," the Keeper said. 
"Were you ever forced to ... cross blades with your own family?" I could not help but ask. 
There was a moment when I thought her emotions might crack, but she raised her gaze to the only skylight of the safe house. There was a brief silence before she sorted out her feelings and reverted to her normal self. "I used a handcannon. It was his." 
Through the skylight, I could see the last rays of dusk reflecting off the clouds. The fireworks erupted, elaborate works of witchcraft woven by the crafters. Seš'qa has enjoyed another day of peace.

- The operation comprises 4 consecutive enemy waves to fight. Defeat all enemy waves to complete the operation.
- Healing Masses will be dropped between the waves. Touch them to restore HP to every teammate.
- There is a resting session between waves, during which the operators are still considered to be in battle.
- The operation has a countdown timer. Once the timer ends or all operators in the team are reduced to ZERO HP, the operation is interrupted.
- Tacticals and consumables cannot be used.

added.2824286048726288192:
DOCUMENT: A Torn Manuscript 
The first mirror witnessed by a babe is usually the eyes of its mother. A mother would gaze at you and softly whisper these words: "Welcome to this world." You opened your eyes to look right back at her, with a focus shared by a navigator attempting to cross an ocean. Once the wet shimmers faded, you would see yourself reflected in her eyes. 
Powerful, weak, hateful, lovely, pure, tainted... You recognized the reflection in the mirror, a reflection of yourself. 
But what if your first mirror was not your mother? What if you opened your eyes before you uttered your first cry? The ocean around you swayed like a swinging cradle, and someone pressed against your fingers. It felt annoying but also calm. You did not know who this person was, but you peered through the turbid fluids, noticed another pair of blurry eyes, and saw your reflection in them. At that very moment, something faster than a bolt of lightning struck through your soul. 
He was born with you. You were the closest friends, yet you always fought and argued. You could not help but peer into his eyes to look at your own reflections... He was the rule and the control by which you were measured against, and the very conduit by which you fed from the world. He, on the other hand, relied upon you, respected you, and envied you. The days passed and your reflection within his eyes grew ever more perfect. 
Then one day it came. The first crack appeared in the mirror. The cracks spread, and the mirror shattered. Your image was gone. The storm descended, the fates murmured, and the oceans drove back into the deep. You gained a scar that would never heal, lost half of your soul, and were forced to raise your weapon against him. It was the only way with which you could pierce the hatred that burned deeper than love and restore what was broken to completion. 
And that moment, you still had not realized — 
— that you were his first mirror. 

DOCUMENT: A Faded News Cutting 
Landbreakers Raid Towns - Seš'qa Planning to Create an Anti-Witchcraft Organization 
The floating city traitor and deserter Jakub Meissen and the Landbreaker raiders he led have recently become a major security threat to the Civilization Band. Jakub's forces have occupied and transformed an Originium farm into their base of operations, sending out raider groups to spread more Originium and raid. 
Just two nights prior, a town at the Band Fringes was attacked by raiders led by a Vampire serving as Jakub's lieutenant. The town, situated at an area not yet claimed by Talosian factions, was poorly defended. However, another Vampire, who hailed from Seš'qa, played a pivotal role in thwarting the raid. Armed with knowledge of Sarkazian witchcraft, the Vampire defender sabotaged the raiders' military hardware and ran his weapon through the chest of the raid leader, forcing the latter to call for retreat. Currently, the raider leader has gone into hiding and Seš'qa is maintaining constant vigilance to track him down. 
Seš'qa regarded Jakub's betrayal as its responsibility. The latest news revealed that the leadership of the floating city is planning to create an organization dedicated to fighting crimes involving Sarkazian witchcraft and apprehending the traitors.

DOCUMENT: Valley IV Monitoring Feed 
1. Originium Byproduct Processing Center (Originium BPPC), Entrance: Clear images. Endfield operators observed. No strange sightings. 
2. Worker Dorms, southeast: UWST crew mates going about their daily activities. No strange sightings. 
3. The Hub Base: Camille Arno identified. Camera zooms in for better tracking. 
4. Aburrey Quarry: Signal lost. Suspected damage to the camera affixed to the Landbreaker; said entity has been attacked and eliminated by the enemy. 
5. Bridge Approach Camp: Several Landbreakers had left or joined the retreating column. Only a few fuzzy videos have been captured. Final position has been abandoned. 
6. United Parking Lot, Originium Power Plant: Focus all cameras on Camille Arno and the Endministrator of Endfield Industries. Monitor the activities of these two individuals. 
...... 
This report proves that an unknown party has deployed a well-hidden monitoring system throughout the region of Valley IV. 

Nobody could have imagined that the Endministrator returned from this trip with several thousand hours worth of video recordings. The discovery, found in an abandoned underground facility, left every analyst in shock. It proves that an organization has been acting from the shadows of Valley IV, silently observing every action of everyone, and pulling secret strings to exert control over the region. 
When the analysts realized that the cameras were implanted within the Landbreakers to avoid detection (Camille mentioned how the perpetrator abandoned them without second thought), they knew why Seš'qa chose to assign an Agaš'mekanaz to the task. Fortunately, after Camille Arno arrived at Valley IV, all the cameras were assigned to monitor his movement and activities. The period of monitoring happens to overlap with Endfield activities in the region. We can safely assume that no pieces of critical intel have been leaked. The lack of leakage is also why none of our operators noticed the unwarranted third-party monitoring. 
After receiving the video feed, operators of the tech division traced the origins to an earlier batch of records (already destroyed) and realized that the organization had begun tracking Camille several decades ago. Though the Keeper himself responded to this finding mildly and appeared to have expected this, the techies sympathized with his plight and promised to give his equipment a suite of upgrades designed to counter covert monitoring measures. When the analysts briefed Camille about their findings, he just leaned against the wall and looked at the screen quietly for quite a while before he raised his eyes and asked: "Say if you were on the other side of the monitoring cameras, staring at a familiar person ... what is most likely to come to mind?" 
The analysts gave all sorts of answers. Camille did not respond to any of them but continued his extended silence. Finally, he left a written note in the debriefing report. 
"So much alike, yet so divergent in essence. Why?" 
He wants the answer to the question. He needs it.

The judges and other tasters of this dish have arrived at a unanimous conclusion: Among the dishes submitted for this cook-off, this ginseng meat stew best exemplified the delicate nature of harmony. The ribs glistened with the dark hues of redjade ginseng. The texture was soft, gently yielding in the mouth without feeling overcooked. Having a bowl of this hearty stew felt like donning a warm coat in winter.
We could obviously tell that the chef has spent a great deal of effort removing the bitterness of the redjade ginseng. Though the astringent taste was gone, the processing also removed the herb's natural and distinctive aromas. Otherwise, the dish would be perfect.

added.5068058273306406927: From the information you recovered, we postulate that these ruins were constructed many years ago, circa the early phases after the outbreak of the First Aggeloi War. For an exact date, we will need you to gather physical samples from the ruins for a chronological analysis aboard Dijiang. Imagine this. In the early days of the First Aggeloi War, a team with an extremely high level of Protocol authority and supervision was ordered to go southwards. They endured all manner of danger, paid a dear price, and reached a place far away from the battlefield just to construct this facility... Perhaps the Aggeloi or major Blight Tides came and turned it into ruins. We postulate that the residents of Qingbo Stockade may know something about these ruins. Unfortunately, if what we know about education and knowledge preservation in Qingbo Stockade is true, written records about this site may have been lost for a while.

added.5932676869511874060:
The judges and other tasters of this dish have arrived at a unanimous conclusion: The chef who prepared this plate of bamboo sprouts and meat knew perfect heat control! The young bamboo sprouts were sweet and crunchy, while the fillet slices were juicy and tender. Quick stir-frying over strong fire perfectly combined the two. The sprouts soaked up the rich gravy as the fillet slices absorbed the refreshing aromas of the bamboo grove.
But everything after the stir-frying revealed the chef's shortcomings. Too many condiments were used. The aroma of redjade ginseng also overpowered the flavors of the primary ingredients. The judges really had to work their palates to peel away the distractions before finally arriving at the refreshing tastes of nature and cozy family stoves. In conclusion, this dish felt like a dime a dozen.

added.7038585790115815443:
DOCUMENT: A Study of Qingbo Martial Arts (Excerpt) 
The origins of Qingbo Martial Arts can be traced back to the village known as Qingbo Stockade in the region of Wuling. It is a practiced tradition of the Stockade residents. However, this collection of fighting techniques is so esoteric that it was not recorded in the Addendum to the Martial Codex. 
One must study the history of Qingbo Stockade to understand the unique features of Qingbo Martial Arts. During the lead-up to the Humiliation War and the several years that followed, a group of Yanese refugees who were determined to distance themselves from factional conflicts and warfare traveled to what became the modern region of Wuling. The Wuling Very Large Rift (VLR) did pose a threat but also provided a barrier against hostile raids and attacks. The refugees thus decided to settle and build a village that eventually grew into Qingbo Stockade. According to some, these Stockaders were the original residents of Wuling. 
From this perspective, Qingbo Stockade seems to be a peaceful sanctuary, but this assumption is far from the truth. The Wuling VLR has created a very hostile environment with large numbers of roaming Aggeloi, fearsome wild beasts, and frequent rift activities. The Stockaders have been constantly fighting against these outside "enemies" and these intense battles gradually gave rise to several combat techniques unique to the Qingbo Stockade. Training in these techniques not only strengthens the body but also heightens one's control over their innate Originium Arts. A few of the moves and skills also highlight the unique features of the Qingbo area. 
An example is the famous "Stride of Surging Spring" that allows martial artists to effortlessly stroll across a flowing river while leaving roiling currents in their wake. At first I mistook it for a form of witchcraft that mentally manipulates flowing water. But the Stride turns out to be an Originium Arts technique that focuses Arts energy into the tips of the feet, then channels it into the water with incredible precision to generate resonating vibrations. Another example is the "Jaunt Across Bamboo Leaves" that is literally leapmancy using flimsy bamboo leaves as support. The technique involves applying a "sliver of resilient pulse" of Originium Arts upon fragile-looking bamboo leaves. Such levels of finesse and control, if honed properly, could unleash a terrifying burst of "Pulser Punch" with barely any movement. 
Watchguard Captain Mi Fu of the Wuling Scientific Developmental District happens to be a practitioner of Qingbo Martial Arts. She described the "Pulser Punch" as a "bolt of lightning". When attacking the enemy, the martial artist should treat the body like a "handcannon", soar like a flying Lung, and descend like a lightning blast. This humble author has personally witnessed Captain Mi Fu in action during a mission to eradicate the Aggeloi. She struck them with such force that her moves seem on par with the Tianshi Lei Fa (Thunder Arts). 
In addition to their powerful martial arts, the Stockaders also established a unique tradition of martial competition. For many years, all martial artists of Qingbo Stockade eagerly challenged each other to see who was the best. On the day of the Arena Showdown, every Stockader, regardless of gender or age, would gather to witness the fights and cheer on their favorite contenders. Captain Mi Fu herself was an undisputed champion of the Stockade before she joined Wuling. Unfortunately, the Stockade arena was undergoing major repairs due to recent damage when the author was penning this section. Deprived of the opportunity to visit the site in person, I could only ask Captain Mi Fu to send me photographs or other image data of the repaired arena. 
I once expressed to Captain Mi Fu my wish to interview the Grand Master of Qingbo Martial Arts, the "River-Tamer" himself. However, the Captain told me that the Grand Master became a hermit and even she did not know where he went. The Captain also showed me a postcard sent by her Senior Master that talked about their travels. The Grand Master's attention was held, for more than an hour, by two kids fighting each other at some alleyway of the Civilization Band. Before leaving, the old man sighed and said: "Trained all my life, but still I envy these kids. They fight when they want and remain friends afterwards. A beautiful sight indeed." 
Finally, I gave Captain Mi Fu a proposal for organizing and compiling information related to Qingbo Martial Arts, and including it within the Continuation for the Addendum to the Martial Codex. The Captain happily agreed and expressed that it was her wish to have more people to protect Wuling with their fists. When that day arrives, she could leave the place (Wuling) and restart her lifelong wish of fighting whenever she wanted.

Heat DMG Dealt <@ba.vup>+{fire_dmg_up:0.0%}</>.
When the wielder's skill applies <#ba.fireinflict>Heat Infliction</>, the wielder gains Physical and Heat DMG Dealt <@ba.vup>+{dmg_up:0.0%}</> for {duration:0}s. When the wielder's skill applies <#ba.firevul>Heat Susceptibility</>, the entire team gains Physical and Heat DMG Dealt <@ba.vup>+{dmg_up2:0.0%}</> for {duration2:0}s.
<@ba.info>The two effects apply separately and do not stack with themselves.</>

added.8605084219856085279:
Hmph. That mad woman! I actually grew up with her and gave her many good beat ups. We don't like each other but I like to give her buggly bruises and she tried to give some back. Mad Mimi was seriously mad back then, but okay. I ain't scared of her. Anyway, she later ran off to Wuling, got herself a badge, and even got promo-ed to be a "Watchguard Captain". She thought it gave her the right to put on a stupid stinkface. Yeah, whatever. She can do that stinkface glare all day. I know what's under her stupid watchguard skin. Mad woman. That's who! 
If I fight her again, we probably end up in a draw. But you'll see. Chief Supreme's gonna win eventually. So what if she's got bigger arms? Don't you forget! I've always been faster and wiser and dodgier. I'll give her absolute wreckage. 
Yeah, and about our Wuling raids, that Mad woman's big trouble. The Fam got everything planned out but we just kept running into her at the depot and warehouse and hidey spots. Like she's already waiting for us. We gave a good fight but Mimi's got a nasty crew and threw many of us in da slammer. Worse, she also tried to undermine our Stockade biz by treating the captured Fam really well. Some of the Fam decided to stay in Wuling 'coz the food and living are better. 
You'll see! I'm gonna pawn that mad woman real good. On my own. Alright? Just you wait, mad woman! Chief Supreme is coming to town and putting you at the baby seat! 
— Tangtang, Supreme Chief of Qingbo Stockade 

Yeh, Fam. Mi Fu threw me in jail. I was hauling phat lewt with the Fam when she suddenly appeared in our way. My legs turned to noodles at once. Then she knocked me around a bit but it wasn't that bad. Anyway, she asked: "Wanna stay in town for a while?" Look, man, I was seeing double and there were stars all around me. I didn't know if I nodded or not, but she really dragged me to town. 
At first I be like, man, I was going straight to hell! But later I got a job at town, roof and bed, plus nice food. Then I learned that Mad Mimi be giving us options. She got that mean face and talked like I owed her money, but Mimi probably had to go plow through tons of burdo-shite trying to get some of the Fam decent gigs. 
Oh yeah, you heard, the loot stashes at the grove that we've been nicking from the Wulingers are actually extra supplies from town. I was a bit confused why bother carry the old and obsolete tools to that place. Turned out they were meant for the Stockade. People say Mad Mimi is super tough on the Stockade Fam, but actually she's always helping and caring in her sneaky way. 
— Anonymous, current citizen of Wuling (former Qingbo Stockade resident)

removed.-8453515455909470637:
Research this to construct <@tech.key>Xiranite Relays</> and <@tech.key>Xiranite Pylons</>.
<@tech.key>Xiranite Relays</> placed outside the Core AIC Area auto-connect to the PAC and any Xiranite power facilities within an 80-meter radius, but cannot connect to other power facilities within the Core AIC Area. When placed inside the Core AIC Area, they only auto-connect to the PAC. They can be used for power transmission and powering facilities within a short range.
<@tech.key>Xiranite Pylons</> placed outside the Core AIC Area auto-connect to the PAC and Xiranite Relays within an 80-meter radius; and to Xiranite Pylons and other power facilities within a 30-meter radius. However, they cannot connect to other power facilities within the Core AIC Area. When placed inside the Core AIC Area, they only auto-connect to the PAC. "Jakub's Legacy" is part of my childhood memories. Reed rye flour seasoned with sizzling hot energized aggagrit, kneaded, baked, then sliced and lightly panfried. Every bite was a resounding crunch... They said the Warlord Jakub left this recipe. But food is food, and food is never wrong.
Carly and the kids came running when they heard about chow. All the young ones were giggling when they had this. They described it like eating dried little twigs, then said it felt warm as though the wind itself was casting the Arts. Finally, they licked their lips and said: "Valley IV got sneaky and placed a little game in my foodie bowl."

Qiming Station is designed with modular assembly and expandability in mind. Its core module and science module were delivered via multiple rocket launches, and these modules were then assembled in orbit using modular docking technology. Qiming Station is furnished with a closed-loop regeneration system capable of recycling and reusing water and air within the modules.
Silently it courses through its orbit, with extended wings cradling hopes and wishes... It carries Dijiang's gaze and projects it across vast distances.

-6070209752904863763.oldText:
<@profile.key>"The Pack sent a tiny pup to talk? Did old Rozzan and his crew kicked the bucket or something?"
Rossi glanced at Erik, who was roaring with laughter along with his crew, and instantly lost any interest she might have had in the leader famously known as the "Cannon of the Wildlands".
This was Rossi's first time handling the clan's ... "business". A week prior, a merchant convoy bound for the Luppino family had been intercepted by Erik. Out of respect for the Pack's name, they hadn't dared touch the couriers, but a sizable portion of the goods meant for return had gone missing. With Catello absent from the clan at the time, Rossi had volunteered, eager to prove her mettle with this particular "deal".
But at that moment, Rossi was starting to regret her decision. This trip was even less interesting than a hunting trip in home turf. The so-called "Cannon of the Wildlands" was wriggling his bulky, bloated body while slapping the table with his hand. From the moment she'd walked in, he'd done nothing but attempt to provoke her.
True strength is reserved, unyielding, never showing a crack. That was a lesson Rossi had learned within the clan. No one, not even Rozzan or the Elder Wolves, ever boasted so crudely about their violence.
"Nonno Rozzan told me this is 'business.' From where I stand, every deal has one side that profits, and another that takes the loss."
Rossi's eyes swept across the tent. The scent of gunpowder drifted to her nose, and she pinpointed where the other side had planted the explosives. The amount was small. Clearly, killing her wasn't the goal. A live wolf seemed far more useful to them.
"The Pack doesn't mind letting our business friends wet their muzzles a little. Unfortunately, I haven't passed my rite of passage yet, so I'm not a true member of the Pack. And I only want profit. I don't do losses."
"Looks like the Pack isn't planning to sit down and talk this out properly, eh?"
A flicker passed through Erik's eyes. The bandits around them grew serious, some already resting their hands on their weapons.
Two swordsmen, two axemen, and probably several casters lying in wait outside the tent. Judging by their positioning inside, it'd be tough to clear them out quickly. So—
Having assessed the situation, Rossi toyed with a fork on the table, waiting for the right moment.
"Listen. From the very first day I set foot in this blighted place, someone's been blabbering non-stop in my years. I can't do this. I can't kill that. I must follow the rules — the Code of the Pack."
Erik's agitation grew. He stared hard at the girl who'd come alone. All these preparations were meant for Rozzan of the Pack. But the old man sent a bratty girl instead... Erik felt insulted.
"The Code? It's written by those who are bigger and holds more power!"
Erik hefted his cannon. The Pack prized their kin. Taking this girl hostage could prove far more useful.
In a flash, a fork shot toward Erik. Rossi sprang from her seat and suddenly disappeared. Erik swatted the utensil away and raised his weapon to aim.
"Behave and be still."
A dagger pressed against the back of his neck.
The surrounding bandits finally drew their weapons, but it was too late. 
"STOP! DON'T GET ANY CLOSER!" Erik yelled, feeling a chilling prick of cold against the back of his neck.
"Counting on those casters at the door to bail you out? But that's odd ... it's way too quiet. Seems my clan wrapped things up even faster than I did. What were you saying again? Oh, right ... the Code..."
The icy tip of the blade seemed to press an inch closer.
"Do you understand what the Code of the Pack is?" Rossi sheathed her dagger.
Erik whirled around, furious. All his careful planning, ruined by a child like this? "I gave the goods to the Bonekrushers. Try and take them back if you have the guts for it! Once they've wiped you out, then I'll be writing up a new Code for this turf!"
"What did you say?"
A killing intent he had never felt before radiated from the girl standing before him.
Erik didn't know the price of bringing up the Bonekrushers around this girl, but he was about to learn. And soon.

Looking at the wrecked tent, Rossi couldn't help but sigh.
"Business is such a hassle."</>

Looking at the wrecked tent, Rossi couldn't help but sigh.

-4394282761300916131.oldText: Phase two of the building of Qingbo Stockade is probably related to environmental engineering. After reading through the writings and records of the sketcher you uploaded, we also checked ambient readings synced with us when you deployed the Sub-PAC. Our analysis revealed that the overall architectural design of Qingbo Stockade was rather similar to outpost expansion during a certain period in our history. Shortly after the First Aggeloi War ended, researchers proposed many plans for building small outposts. These designs focused on maximizing the use of local topography, adapting to the local environment, and low-cost construction methods to intercept, channel, and divert Blight masses... Qingbo Stockade happens to match this approach. "Water falls from the sky" may seem like an esoteric line, but Qingbo Stockade actually lives up to this name with its high-rise buildings. We can assume, with good reason, that an individual knowledgeable in such architectural approaches lived amongst the Stockaders during that time.
-4394282761300916131.newText: Phase two of the building of Qingbo Stockade is probably related to environmental engineering. After reading through the writings and records of the sketcher you uploaded, we also checked ambient readings synced with us when you deployed the Sub-PAC. Our analysis revealed that the overall architectural design of Qingbo Stockade was rather similar to outpost expansion during a certain period in our history. Shortly after the First Aggeloi War ended, researchers proposed many plans for building small outposts. These designs focused on maximizing the use of local topography, adapting to the local environment, and low-cost construction methods to intercept, channel, and divert Blight masses... Qingbo Stockade happens to match this approach. "Water cascades from the heavens above" may seem like an esoteric line, but Qingbo Stockade actually lives up to this name with its high-rise buildings. We can assume, with good reason, that an individual knowledgeable in such architectural approaches lived amongst the Stockaders during that time.

4876804338743582626.oldText:
<@intru.bold>- Protocol Pass -</> 
Every item on the Protocol Pass is gathered from official operator interviews conducted by the Arsenal Engineers. These items include support projects that everyone hopes to see. 
Activating each seasonal Protocol Pass unlocks the [Basic Supply] tier by default. 
The [Protocol Customized] tier can be purchased, while the [Originium Supply] tier can be unlocked by spending [Origeometry]. 

<@intru.bold>- Protocol Pass EXP and Leveling -</> 
There are 2 major ways to earn Protocol Pass EXP, which then levels up the Protocol Pass and grants you rewards. 
· Complete Pass Missions (some missions will become available after meeting certain criteria). 
· Take part in [Operational Manual - Daily Tasks] and claim the max daily Activity Points rewards. 
※ If max Activity Points is not reached for the day, 1 Vacation Tag will be activated at 04:00 (Server Time) of the following day. Up to 3 tags can be activated. 
※ After earning max Activity Points and claiming the rewards, 1 activated Vacation Tag will be consumed to give double Pass EXP (Daily task rewards are not affected by this). 

Even after maxing the seasonal Protocol Pass level, you can still earn Pass EXP with the aforementioned methods and acquire cyclical rewards. There is no limit to earning Pass EXP that exceed the level cap. 

※ Special EXP bonus details: Unlocking [Originium Supply] / [Protocol Customized] tiers of the seasonal Protocol Pass grants +3% / +3% Pass EXP bonus respectively, for a total of +6% Pass EXP bonus when both tiers are unlocked. 

<@intru.bold>- Protocol Pass Missions Details -</> 
· Weekly Missions: During the Protocol Pass season, weekly missions become available every 7 days. Weekly missions will be reset when the pass season ends. 
· Horizons Missions: These are a special type of Protocol Pass missions that are permanently available. Horizons Missions progress will not be reset when the pass season ends, meaning that these missions can be performed during any pass season to claim their rewards. When all Horizons Missions are completed, its special screen will be closed at 04:00 (Server Time) of the following day. 

<@intru.bold>- Protocol Pass Weapons Details -</>
For each pass season, the [Originium Supply] and [Protocol Customized] tiers each contain a special weapon supply.
The weapon supplies and the list of weapons that you can choose from are specific to the pass season. In other words, the list of weapons for weapon supplies from the current season may differ from those of previous seasons. However, once a season weapon supply is received, its list of weapons will not be changed.
[Protocolized Weapon Patterns] can raise the potential of any weapon provided by Protocol Pass weapon supplies, including weapon supplies from future pass seasons. 

<@intru.bold>- Special Rules -</>
When the Protocol Pass season ends, any unclaimed pass level rewards shall be sent to your Mail.
For excessive purchases of [Protocol Customized] due to special circumstances, rewards from [Protocol Customized] tier will not stack. Instead, an amount of [Origeometry] based on the value of the purchase will be returned to you. 

<@intru.bold>- Exchange Crate-o-Surprise Details -</> 
[Exchange Crates-o-Surprise] are automatically opened when acquired. Crate contents are fixed: a random Oroberyl reward and a random progression material reward. 

※ Random Rewards Drop Rates 
· Exchange Crate-o-Surprise [M] 
Oroberyls: Oroberyl×20 (5%) / Oroberyl×15 (35%) / Oroberyl×10 (60%) 
Progression Materials: T-Creds×5000 (15%) / T-Creds×3000 (10%) / Elementary Cognitive Carrier×10 (15%) / Elementary Cognitive Carrier×5 (10%) / Arms INSP Set×2 (15%) / Arms INSP Set×1 (10%) / Protohedron×2 (15%) / Protohedron×1 (10%) 

<@intru.bold>- Cycling Crate-o-Surprise Details -</> 
[Cycling Crates-o-Surprise] come in 2 types: (1) Cycling Crate-o-Surprise [M] and (2) Cycling Crate-o-Surprise [L]. 
[Cycling Crates-o-Surprise] are only acquired from the cycling rewards, and are automatically opened when acquired. Crate contents are fixed: a random Oroberyl reward, a random progression material reward, and a random Sanity Usage Permit reward. 

※ Random Rewards Drop Rates 
· Cycling Crate-o-Surprise [M] 
Oroberyls: Oroberyl×20 (5%) / Oroberyl×15 (35%) / Oroberyl×10 (60%) 
Progression Materials: T-Creds×5000 (15%) / T-Creds×3000 (10%) / Elementary Cognitive Carrier×10 (15%) / Elementary Cognitive Carrier×5 (10%) / Arms INSP Set×2 (15%) / Arms INSP Set×1 (10%) / Protohedron×2 (15%) / Protohedron×1 (10%) 
Sanity Usage Permits: Sanity Usage Permit×1 (90%) / Sanity Usage Permit×2 (10%) 
· Cycling Crate-o-Surprise [L] 
Oroberyls: Oroberyl×30 (5%) / Oroberyl×25 (35%) / Oroberyl×20 (60%) 
Progression Materials: T-Creds×12000 (12.5%) / T-Creds×7500 (12.5%) / Advanced Cognitive Carrier×2 (12.5%) / Advanced Cognitive Carrier×1 (12.5%) / Arms INSP Set×5 (12.5%) / Arms INSP Set×3 (12.5%) / Protohedron×5 (12.5%) / Protohedron×3 (12.5%) 
Sanity Usage Permits: Sanity Usage Permit×3 (90%) / Sanity Usage Permit×4 (10%)

7234092887806552613.oldText:
<@intru.bold>- Participation Requirements -</> 
<@gray.note>· Complete the main mission [Chapter Ⅱ Process Ⅱ: Where Danger Sleeps].</> 

<@intru.bold>- Event Rules -</> 
<@gray.note>· During the event, complete various missions, produce support goods, and trade them with the outpost for generous rewards.</> 
<@gray.note>· The event is divided into 3 phases. Completing the Phase 1 mission gives rewards as well as a limited-time formula for a special support goods. Use the AIC Factory to produce and trade the said goods with the outpost to earn [Wuling Stock Bills] and [Fruits of Altruism Certs], the latter can be spent at the Goods Exchange for useful goods.</> 
<@gray.note>· On day 8 of the event, the Goods Exchange will have new exchangeable goods.</> 
<@gray.note>· Completing the Phase 1 mission also starts the Phase 2 mission. Completing the Phase 2 mission gives rewards and an advanced limited-time formula for an advanced type of special support goods. Produce and trade the said goods with the outpost for more [Wuling Stock Bills] and [Fruits of Altruism Certs], the latter can be spent at the Goods Exchange for useful goods.</> 
<@gray.note>· After reaching certain Fruits of Altruism Certs criteria, the mission of the last Phase will begin. Complete this mission to earn rewards.</> 
<@gray.note>· The event ends on May 13 at 16:00 (server time). Any support goods already produced but not yet traded to the outpost will be auto-converted to [Wuling Stock Bills] and [Fruits of Altruism Certs] and sent to the in-game Mail. If the amount of [Wuling Stock Bills] from this conversion exceeds the stock bill limit, the excess portion will be auto-converted to [Fruits of Altruism Certs].</> 
<@gray.note>· After the event ends, the Goods Exchange will remain open for a while so you can trade for the remaining supplies. Please complete all your exchanges before May 19 at 04:00 (server time).</>
